---
title: "Model Selection"
---

Load data for modeling

```{r}

library(colorEvoHelpers)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(phyr)
library(dplyr)
library(car)
library(performance)
library(MuMIn)
library(buildmer)
library(glmmTMB)
library(splines)
library(sjPlot)
library(ggeffects)
library(brms)
library(sjPlot)

d <- readRDS("_targets/objects/d_sp_cell")
```

Latitude model selection

```{r}

library(lme4)
library(ggeffects)
library(splines)
library(sjPlot)
library(ggplot2)

l_m1 <- lmer(black_20 ~ cell_lat_sc * onset_sc + lightness_skew_sc + (1|species) + (1|cell), data = d)
l_m2 <- lmer(black_20 ~ ns(cell_lat_sc, 2) * onset_sc + lightness_skew_sc + (1|species) + (1|cell), data = d)
l_m3 <- lmer(black_20 ~ cell_lat_sc + onset_sc + lightness_skew_sc + (1|species) + (1|cell), data = d)
l_m4 <- lmer(black_20 ~ ns(cell_lat_sc, 2) + onset_sc + lightness_skew_sc + (1|species) + (1|cell), data = d)

AIC(l_m1)
AIC(l_m2)
AIC(l_m3)
AIC(l_m4)

summary(l_m2)
summary(l_m4)

saveRDS(l_m1, "models/l_m1.rds")
saveRDS(l_m2, "models/l_m2.rds")
saveRDS(l_m3, "models/l_m3.rds")
saveRDS(l_m4, "models/l_m4.rds")
```

Buildmer procedure for pairs of climatic variables

```{r}

myBuildmer <- function(climate_vars = c("tmean_sc", "srad_sc"),data,vifs=TRUE,return_model=TRUE) {
  d <- data
  # ensure needed packages
  if (!requireNamespace("splines", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'splines' is required but not installed.")
  }
  if (!requireNamespace("sjPlot", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'sjPlot' is required but not installed.")
  }
  
  # 1. Build spline term strings
  spline_terms <- paste0("ns(", climate_vars, ", 2)")
  
  # 2. Assemble fixed‐effect RHS: (onset + splines...)^2
  fixed_parts <- c("onset_sc", spline_terms)
  rhs <- paste0("(", paste(fixed_parts, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
  
  # 3. Full formula with random effects
  full_formula <- as.formula(paste(
    "black_20 ~",
    rhs,
    "+ (1 | species) + (1 | cell)"
  ))
  
  # 4. Build 'include' list for splines
  include_list <- setNames(
    lapply(spline_terms, function(term) {
      paste0(term, 1:2)
    }),
    spline_terms
  )
  
  # 5. Fit with buildglmmTMB
  m_build <- buildglmmTMB(
    full_formula,
    data = d,
    family = gaussian(), #beta_family()
    buildmerControl = buildmerControl(
      direction = "backward",
      include  = include_list,
      crit     = "AIC",
      elim     = "AIC"
    )
  )
  print(summary(m_build@model))
  
    # 6. Quick lmer for collinearity check
  #    black_20 ~ var1 + var2 + … + (1|species) + (1|cell)
  test_formula <- as.formula(
    paste0("black_20 ~ ", paste(climate_vars, collapse = " + "), 
           " + lightness_skew_sc + (1 | species) + (1 | cell)")
  )
  test_mod <- lme4::lmer(test_formula, data = d)
  
  if(vifs){
    # 7. Compute & print VIFs
    vif_vals <- car::vif(test_mod)
    message("VIFs for climate predictors in simple lmer:\n")
    print(vif_vals) 
  }
  if(return_model){
    return(m_build@model)
  }
  return(m_build)
}

m1_build <- myBuildmer(climate_vars = c("tmean_sc", "srad_sc"),data=d,return_model=FALSE)
m1 <- m1_build@model
plot_model(m1,type="pred",terms=c("tmean_sc","onset_sc"))
plot_model(m1,type="pred",terms=c("srad_sc"))
plot_model(m1,type="pred",terms=c("onset_sc"))

m2_build <- myBuildmer(climate_vars = c("tmean_sc", "vp_sc"),data=d,return_model=FALSE)
m2 <- m2_build@model
plot_model(m2,type="pred",terms=c("tmean_sc","onset_sc"))
plot_model(m2,type="pred",terms=c("vp_sc"))
plot_model(m2,type="pred",terms=c("onset_sc"))

m3_build <- myBuildmer(climate_vars = c("srad_sc", "vp_sc"),data=d,return_model=FALSE)
m3 <- m3_build@model
plot_model(m3,type="pred",terms=c("vp_sc","onset_sc"))
plot_model(m3,type="pred",terms=c("srad_sc"))
plot_model(m3,type="pred",terms=c("onset_sc"))

plot_model(m1, type = "pred", terms = c("onset_sc")) + scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0.2, 0.4, by = 0.1), limits = c(0.2, 0.4)) + labs(x = "Phenological Onset", y = NULL, title = NULL)
```

```{r}

saveRDS(m1, "models/m1.rds")
saveRDS(m2, "models/m2.rds")
saveRDS(m3, "models/m3.rds")
```

```{r}

buildmerDecisiveness <- function(x, crit_col = NULL, step_col = NULL) {
  if (!inherits(x, "buildmer")) stop("Provide a 'buildmer' object.")
  res <- x@p$results
  if (is.null(res) || !nrow(res)) stop("No results table found in x@p$results.")
  nm <- names(res)

  # 1) Criterion column
  if (is.null(crit_col)) {
    crit_candidates <- nm[grepl("^(AIC|BIC|Deviance|deviance|IC|crit)$", nm)]
    if (length(crit_candidates) == 0) stop("Couldn't auto-detect a criterion column. Pass crit_col='AIC' (or 'BIC', etc.).")
    crit_col <- crit_candidates[1]
  }
  if (!crit_col %in% nm) stop("crit_col '", crit_col, "' not found in results; available: ", paste(nm, collapse=", "))

  # Keep finite rows
  res <- res[is.finite(res[[crit_col]]), , drop = FALSE]
  if (!"term" %in% nm) stop("No 'term' column found; cannot summarise.")

  # 2) If you have an explicit step/round column, use it
  if (!is.null(step_col)) {
    if (!step_col %in% names(res)) stop("step_col '", step_col, "' not in results.")
    step_vec <- res[[step_col]]
  } else {
    # 3) Reconstruct steps by grouping consecutive blocks where the set size of candidate terms
    #    stays constant, assuming buildmer lists one row per candidate at each elimination step.
    #    We detect block boundaries when the next row repeats a previously seen term pattern size change.
    #    A robust heuristic: identify runs between decreases in the *max cumulative count* of a given term.
    #    Simpler and effective: assume each step is a block where 'term' does not repeat within the block.
    #    Start a new step whenever a term repeats within the current block or when the set size shrinks.

    step_vec <- integer(nrow(res))
    current_terms <- character(0)
    step_id <- 1L
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(res))) {
      term_i <- as.character(res$term[i])
      if (term_i %in% current_terms) {
        # repeated term → start a new block
        step_id <- step_id + 1L
        current_terms <- term_i
      } else {
        current_terms <- c(current_terms, term_i)
      }
      step_vec[i] <- step_id
      # Heuristic: if the next row's term set looks like it's starting a new evaluation (often happens
      # after the best candidate is "chosen"), we’ll also bump the step at the next iteration
      if (i < nrow(res)) {
        # if next row reuses many earlier terms after a presumed drop, we reset next loop naturally
        # (the repetition rule above will handle it)
      }
    }
  }

  # 4) Summarise per step
  # Requires dplyr
  if (!requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE)) stop("Please install 'dplyr'.")
  library(dplyr)

  out <- tibble::as_tibble(res) %>%
    mutate(..step = step_vec) %>%
    group_by(..step) %>%
    arrange(.data[[crit_col]], .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    summarise(
      n_candidates   = n(),
      best_term      = first(term),
      best_crit      = first(.data[[crit_col]]),
      second_crit    = nth(.data[[crit_col]], 2, default = NA_real_),
      delta_to_second = second_crit - best_crit,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      judgement = case_when(
        is.na(delta_to_second) ~ "only one option",
        delta_to_second < 2    ~ "borderline",
        delta_to_second < 4    ~ "modest",
        TRUE                   ~ "strong"
      )
    )
  out
}

buildmerDecisiveness(m1_build, crit_col = "AIC")
buildmerDecisiveness(m2_build, crit_col = "AIC")
buildmerDecisiveness(m3_build, crit_col = "AIC")
```

```{r}

cor.test(d$lightness_skew_sc, d$latitude)
cor.test(d$lightness_skew_sc, d$longitude)
cor.test(d$lightness_skew_sc, d$tmean)
cor.test(d$lightness_skew_sc, d$srad)
cor.test(d$lightness_skew_sc, d$vp)
cor.test(d$lightness_skew_sc, d$black_20)
cor.test(d$lightness_skew_sc, d$mean_lightness)
```

```{r}


# Load required library
library(lme4)
library(broom.mixed)
library(sjPlot)
# Test if darker species have more positive lightness skew
# (controlling for species-level variation with random effects)

# Model 1: Mean lightness predicting skew
model_lightness <- lmer(lightness_skew_sc ~ mean_lightness_sc + (1|species), 
                       data = d)

# Model 2: Black pixels predicting skew  
model_black <- lmer(lightness_skew_sc ~ black_20_sc + (1|species), 
                   data = d)

plot_model(model_black)
# Model 3: Combined model
model_combined <- lmer(lightness_skew_sc ~ mean_lightness_sc + black_20_sc + (1|species), 
                      data = d)

# Print results
cat("=== Model 1: Mean Lightness ===\n")
print(summary(model_lightness))
cat("\n=== Model 2: Black Pixels ===\n")
print(summary(model_black))
cat("\n=== Model 3: Combined ===\n")
print(summary(model_combined))

# Extract key statistics
cat("\n=== Key Results ===\n")
cat("Mean Lightness Effect (Model 1):\n")
print(tidy(model_lightness, effects = "fixed"))

cat("\nBlack Pixels Effect (Model 2):\n")
print(tidy(model_black, effects = "fixed"))

cat("\nCombined Effects (Model 3):\n")
print(tidy(model_combined, effects = "fixed"))

# Check if the relationship holds after accounting for species
cat("\n=== Interpretation ===\n")
lightness_coef <- fixef(model_lightness)["mean_lightness_sc"]
black_coef <- fixef(model_black)["black_20_sc"]

if(lightness_coef < 0) {
  cat("✓ Darker species (lower mean lightness) have more positive skew\n")
} else {
  cat("✗ No evidence that darker species have more positive skew\n")
}

if(black_coef > 0) {
  cat("✓ Species with more black pixels have more positive skew\n")
} else {
  cat("✗ No evidence that species with more black have more positive skew\n")
}
```

Supplemental analysis with skewed data points removed

```{r}

# Wrapper to refit a glmmTMB model with phylogeny via phyr
refitWithPhylo <- function(glmmTMB_model, data, tree) {
  # Extract formula and modify for phyr syntax
  f <- formula(glmmTMB_model)
  f_char <- as.character(f)
  
  # Replace (1 | species) with (1 | species__)
  f_char[3] <- gsub("\\(1 \\| species\\)", "(1 | species__)", f_char[3])
  new_formula <- as.formula(paste(f_char[2], "~", f_char[3]))
  
  phyr::pglmm(
    new_formula,
    data = data,
    cov_ranef = list(species = tree),
    REML = FALSE
  )
}

# Compare
m1_phylo <- refitWithPhylo(m1, d, tree_trimmed)
summary(m1_phylo)
summary(m1)
```
